topic: Cloudflare Agents Week 2026: Dynamic Workers, Sandboxes GA, and Project Think

competitors.title: Cloudflare Agents Week Blog Series
key_points: Announced April 15-19, 2026: Project Think, Dynamic Workers, Browser Run (formerly Browser Rendering), Agent Lee
Dynamic Workers: ~100x faster, up to 100x more memory-efficient than traditional containers
Browser Run: Live View, Human in the Loop, CDP access, session recordings, 4x higher concurrency limits
Agent Lee: in-dashboard agent using sandboxed TypeScript to troubleshoot and manage Cloudflare stack
Cloudflare Email Service: public beta for agents to send, receive, and process email natively
Registrar API beta: agents can search, check availability, register domains from editor/terminal
AI Search primitive: create instances dynamically, upload files, hybrid retrieval and relevance boosting
Deploy Postgres and MySQL databases with PlanetScale + Workers
Agents SDK evolution: from lightweight primitives to batteries-included platform

competitors.title: Project Think: building the next generation of AI agents on Cloudflare
key_points: Precoded the next edition of Agents SDK — from primitives to platform for AI agents that think, act, persist
Core primitives: Fibers (durable execution), Facets (sub-agents), Sessions (persistent conversations)
Sandboxed execution: agents can run generated code in secure, isolated environments
Execution Ladder: Tier 0 (Workspace/SQLite), Tier 1 (Dynamic Worker no network), Tier 2 (Dynamic Worker + npm)
worker-bundler fetches packages from npm registry and bundles with esbuild at runtime
Dynamic Workers begin with almost no ambient authority (globalOutbound: null)
Capabilities granted explicitly through bindings — resource by resource
Facets have RPC latency comparable to function calls, not HTTP round-trips
Project Think is in preview as of April 2026; APIs may change as feedback incorporated

competitors.title: Introducing Agent Lee - a new interface to the Cloudflare stack
key_points: Agent Lee shifts Cloudflare's interface from manual tab-switching to a single prompt
Uses sandboxed TypeScript, helps troubleshoot and manage stack as grounded technical collaborator
Runs directly in dashboard, can be invoked from dashboard search or chat interface
Can inspect configurations, suggest fixes, run diagnostics, and manage resources
Example: 'Why is my website slow?' → Agent Lee analyzes config, suggests optimizations

competitors.title: Dynamic Workers: sandboxed execution for AI agents
key_points: Dynamic Workers enable AI agents to generate and execute TypeScript code safely
Built on Workers runtime with additional security boundaries and resource limits
No ambient authority: start with globalOutbound: null, capabilities added via bindings
Execution contexts: Tier 0 (in-process SQLite), Tier 1 (isolated no-network), Tier 2 (network-capable)
Performance: ~100x faster and up to 100x more memory-efficient than traditional containers
Package bundling: worker-bundler fetches npm packages at runtime and bundles with esbuild
Security: runs in separate isolate from parent Worker, limited memory and execution time

competitors.title: Browser Run: give your agents a browser
key_points: Browser Rendering rebranded to Browser Run with expanded capabilities
Live View: watch agents interact with websites in real-time
Human in the Loop: pause execution for human verification or input
Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) access for advanced browser automation
Session recordings: capture and replay agent interactions
4x higher concurrency limits for AI agents
Supports headless and headed modes, screen capture, and JavaScript evaluation

stats.fact: Cloudflare internal AI engineering stack processed 20 million requests routed through AI Gateway during Agents Week
241 billion tokens processed through Cloudflare Workers AI (Agents Week 2026)
More than 3,683 internal Cloudflare users served inference on Workers AI during Agents Week
Dynamic Workers are ~100x faster and up to 100x more memory-efficient than traditional containers
Cloudflare Email Service now in public beta, enabling agents to send/receive email natively
Registrar API beta allows agents to search, check availability, and register domains at cost
Browser Run offers 4x higher concurrency limits than previous Browser Rendering service
Project Think is in preview as of April 2026, with APIs subject to change based on feedback

angles: From primitives to platform: How Cloudflare's AI agent stack evolved from SDK to full ecosystem
The security-first approach: Dynamic Workers and sandboxed execution for AI-generated code
Agent Lee: Transforming cloud management from manual clicks to conversational interfaces
Multi-channel agents: Expanding beyond chat to email, browser automation, and domain registration
Project Think vs traditional serverless: Why durable execution beats stateless for multi-step agents
Practical guide: Building your first agent with Project Think, Dynamic Workers, and Agent Lee
Enterprise adoption: How Cloudflare's internal usage validates the agent platform at scale
